title: ophrys garganica subsp. caloptera
intro: Ophrys garganica subsp. caloptera, also known as the bee orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its unique and intricate flower shape that resembles a female bee. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Ophrys garganica subsp. caloptera include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
